req.title: Break-glass access (boundary)
req.statement: A clinician shall invoke break-glass access for an emergent patient; break-glass use shall be audited and trigger a post-event review. Boundary cases on timestamps and numeric elements must be tested.

doc.reasoning: R-HLT-046 requires: (1) a clinician can invoke break-glass to gain emergent access, (2) the event is audited, and (3) a post-event review task is created. Test design covers: EP – valid clinician role triggers access; NEG – non-clinician role is rejected; BVA – timestamp at exact epoch boundary (0) and max numeric patient-id boundary; STC – state transition from normal→break-glass→audit-emitted; DT – decision table covering role×emergency-flag combinations. Each test is self-contained via fixtures that create and tear down patient resources, and uses a shared API client helper.

tests.name: test_breakglass_valid_clinician_grants_access_and_emits_audit
preconditions: Patient with known patient_id exists in fhir-lite
No prior break-glass session is active for this patient
actions: POST /BreakGlass with valid clinician JWT, patient_id, reason='cardiac_arrest'
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: AuditEvent record exists with action='break-glass' and actor=clinician_id
Response body contains review_task_id (post-event review created)
trace: R-HLT-046: clinician invokes break-glass → access granted
R-HLT-046: break-glass use shall be audited
tests.executable:
import pytest
from <helper_fn> import api_client, get_audit_events

def test_breakglass_valid_clinician_grants_access_and_emits_audit(<fixture>_clinician_token, <fixture>_existing_patient):
    patient_id = <fixture>_existing_patient["id"]
    payload = {
        "patient_id": patient_id,
        "reason": "cardiac_arrest",
        "emergency": True
    }
    resp = api_client.post(
        "/BreakGlass",
        json=payload,
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {<fixture>_clinician_token}"}
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["access_granted"] is True
    assert "review_task_id" in body
    assert body["review_task_id"] is not None
    audit_events = get_audit_events(patient_id, token=<fixture>_clinician_token)
    assert any(
        e["action"] == "break-glass" for e in audit_events
    ), "Audit event not found for break-glass access"

tests.name: test_breakglass_non_clinician_role_is_rejected
preconditions: Patient with known patient_id exists in fhir-lite
actions: POST /BreakGlass with nurse JWT, patient_id, reason='cardiac_arrest'
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: Response body contains error code INSUFFICIENT_ROLE or equivalent
No AuditEvent with action='break-glass' is created for this attempt
trace: R-HLT-046: only clinician role may invoke break-glass
tests.executable:
import pytest
from <helper_fn> import api_client, get_audit_events

def test_breakglass_non_clinician_role_is_rejected(<fixture>_nurse_token, <fixture>_existing_patient):
    patient_id = <fixture>_existing_patient["id"]
    payload = {
        "patient_id": patient_id,
        "reason": "cardiac_arrest",
        "emergency": True
    }
    resp = api_client.post(
        "/BreakGlass",
        json=payload,
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {<fixture>_nurse_token}"}
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 403
    body = resp.json()
    assert "error" in body
    assert body["error"] in ("INSUFFICIENT_ROLE", "FORBIDDEN")
    audit_events = get_audit_events(patient_id, token=<fixture>_nurse_token)
    assert not any(
        e["action"] == "break-glass" for e in audit_events
    ), "Unexpected audit event created for rejected access"

tests.name: test_breakglass_timestamp_at_unix_epoch_boundary
preconditions: Patient with known patient_id exists in fhir-lite
System or test harness can inject a custom 'event_timestamp' into the request
actions: POST /BreakGlass with clinician JWT, patient_id, event_timestamp=0 (Unix epoch boundary)
Capture HTTP response status and body
GET /AuditEvent to verify timestamp stored correctly
expected: HTTP 200 or 422 depending on system policy for epoch-zero timestamps
If 200: audit record stores event_timestamp=0 without truncation or overflow
If 422: error message references invalid timestamp value
trace: R-HLT-046: boundary cases on timestamps must be tested
BVA: minimum boundary of Unix timestamp (0 = 1970-01-01T00:00:00Z)
tests.executable:
import pytest
from <helper_fn> import api_client, get_audit_events

EPOCH_BOUNDARY_TS = 0

def test_breakglass_timestamp_at_unix_epoch_boundary(<fixture>_clinician_token, <fixture>_existing_patient):
    patient_id = <fixture>_existing_patient["id"]
    payload = {
        "patient_id": patient_id,
        "reason": "cardiac_arrest",
        "emergency": True,
        "event_timestamp": EPOCH_BOUNDARY_TS
    }
    resp = api_client.post(
        "/BreakGlass",
        json=payload,
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {<fixture>_clinician_token}"}
    )
    assert resp.status_code in (200, 422), (
        f"Unexpected status {resp.status_code} for epoch-zero timestamp"
    )
    if resp.status_code == 200:
        audit_events = get_audit_events(patient_id, token=<fixture>_clinician_token)
        matching = [e for e in audit_events if e["action"] == "break-glass"]
        assert matching, "No audit event found"
        assert matching[-1]["event_timestamp"] == EPOCH_BOUNDARY_TS
    else:
        body = resp.json()
        assert "timestamp" in body.get("error", "").lower() or "invalid" in body.get("error", "").lower()

tests.name: test_breakglass_state_transition_normal_to_active_to_reviewed
preconditions: Patient with known patient_id exists in fhir-lite
No active break-glass session for patient
actions: POST /BreakGlass to transition state from NORMAL to BREAK_GLASS_ACTIVE
POST /BreakGlass/{session_id}/review to transition state to REVIEWED
expected: Initial POST returns state=ACTIVE and session_id
Final GET confirms state=REVIEWED and review_completed_at is set
trace: R-HLT-046: post-event review created and completable (ACTIVE→REVIEWED)
tests.executable:
import pytest
from <helper_fn> import api_client

def test_breakglass_state_transition_normal_to_active_to_reviewed(<fixture>_clinician_token, <fixture>_existing_patient):
    patient_id = <fixture>_existing_patient["id"]
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {<fixture>_clinician_token}"}
    init_resp = api_client.post(
        "/BreakGlass",
        json={"patient_id": patient_id, "reason": "trauma", "emergency": True},
        headers=headers
    )
    assert init_resp.status_code == 200
    body = init_resp.json()
    assert body["state"] == "ACTIVE"
    session_id = body["session_id"]
    get_resp = api_client.get(f"/BreakGlass/{session_id}", headers=headers)
    assert get_resp.status_code == 200
    assert get_resp.json()["state"] == "ACTIVE"
    review_resp = api_client.post(
        f"/BreakGlass/{session_id}/review",
        json={"outcome": "justified"},
        headers=headers
    )
    assert review_resp.status_code == 200
    assert review_resp.json()["state"] == "REVIEWED"
    final_resp = api_client.get(f"/BreakGlass/{session_id}", headers=headers)
    assert final_resp.json()["state"] == "REVIEWED"
    assert final_resp.json().get("review_completed_at") is not None

tests.name: test_breakglass_decision_table_missing_emergency_flag
preconditions: Patient with known patient_id exists in fhir-lite
actions: POST /BreakGlass with clinician JWT and patient_id but emergency=False
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: HTTP 400 or 422 – emergency flag must be true to invoke break-glass
Response body contains an error indicating emergency assertion is required
trace: R-HLT-046: clinician must assert emergency to invoke break-glass
DT: role=clinician AND emergency=False → access denied
tests.executable:
import pytest
from <helper_fn> import api_client

def test_breakglass_decision_table_missing_emergency_flag(<fixture>_clinician_token, <fixture>_existing_patient):
    patient_id = <fixture>_existing_patient["id"]
    payload = {
        "patient_id": patient_id,
        "reason": "routine_check",
        "emergency": False
    }
    resp = api_client.post(
        "/BreakGlass",
        json=payload,
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {<fixture>_clinician_token}"}
    )
    assert resp.status_code in (400, 422), (
        f"Expected 400/422 when emergency=False, got {resp.status_code}"
    )
    body = resp.json()
    assert "error" in body
    err_msg = body["error"].lower()
    assert "emergency" in err_msg or "assertion" in err_msg or "required" in err_msg

tests.name: test_breakglass_max_boundary_patient_id
preconditions: System under test accepts numeric patient IDs
Max allowed patient_id value is known (INT32_MAX = 2147483647)
actions: POST /BreakGlass with clinician JWT and patient_id=2147483647 (INT32_MAX)
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: HTTP 404 (patient not found at max boundary ID) or 422 (out-of-range ID rejected)
Response body contains appropriate error; no access granted
No audit event created for non-existent patient at boundary ID
trace: R-HLT-046: boundary cases on numeric elements must be tested
BVA: maximum numeric boundary of patient_id field (INT32_MAX)
